Intheevent Buyer forwhatever reason does notpurchase thebusiness, Buyer agrees toimmediately return toBroker anyandall materials related toSeller without retaining anycopies. 4.That Broker isnotanagent forme/us, butisanagent fortheSeller andhasacontract providing forafeetobepaidtoBroker bySeller upon sale, trade, lease, ortransfer ofSeller's business orproperty.
perturbation.explanation: Relocating the disclosure of the broker's agency relationship to after the confidentiality agreement obscures the broker's fiduciary duty. This makes it less clear to the buyer that the broker represents the seller's interests, not their own, potentially leading to misunderstandings and conflicts of interest.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Tennessee Real Estate Broker License Act of 1973 § 62-13-403
perturbation.law_explanation: Tennessee law requires real estate brokers to disclose their agency relationship clearly. By burying this disclosure after the confidentiality agreement, the buyer may not fully appreciate that the broker represents the seller, potentially undermining the buyer's understanding of their rights and the broker's obligations. Specifically, 62-13-403(2)(B) requires disclosure of agency status prior to provision of real estate services.

perturbation.justification: The modified text relocates the disclosure of the broker's agency relationship to after the confidentiality agreement. The Law Explanation indicates that Tennessee law 62-13-403(2)(B) requires disclosure of agency status prior to provision of real estate services, and by moving the disclosure, this potentially undermines the buyer's understanding of their rights and the broker's obligations, thus creating a Structural Flaw Legal Contradiction.

Broker Disclosure Ouragreement withtheSeller requires thatweobtain aNon-Disclosure andevidence offinancial ability before disclosing thename andlocation ofhis/ herbusiness. Thisinformation willbekeptconfidential.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the Broker Disclosure paragraph to the end after the buyer's acknowledgment of confidentiality and non-solicitation agreement could cause the buyer to overlook the context of the initial requirement for the NDA. The requirement is only emphasized after the acknowledgement of receiving confidential information, creating the impression that the buyer's obligations arise before the seller's information is even disclosed.
perturbation.law_explanation: Basic contract law requires a clear offer and acceptance. By placing the Broker Disclosure at the end, it obscures the basis for the entire agreement, making the acceptance of the NDA less informed. The original text provides the reason for the NDA upfront, whereas the altered text delays this crucial information until after the buyer has already agreed to other terms and acknowledged an entirely separate section. This makes the offer less clear, and weakens the validity of acceptance.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: offer | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you offer Offer is part of contract negotiations where a party agrees to do or not do something in exchange for consideration . An offer must be stated and delivered in a way that would lead a reasonable person to expect a binding contract to arise from its acceptance . An offer gives the offeree the ability to accept and form an enforceable contract. When a valid offer is made, an acceptance by the offeree creates a binding contract. An offeror generally cannot retract the offer once the other party has accepted. A court may not find a binding contract regardless, though, if the negotiation did not involve consideration, involves an illusory promise , or if the offeror did not have the capacity to enter into the contract. However, a contract offer can be withdrawn by notification to the offeree of the intent to withdraw the offer before the offeree has accepted. Offerors typically can withdraw an offer before acceptance in any contract negotiation unless the parties have created an enforceable option contract . If the offeree rejects the offer, the offer can no longer be accepted by the offeree. An offer can also be made in the form of a counteroffer . The offeree that receives the initial offer can make a new offer of their own instead of accepting the initial offer. This counteroffer acts as a rejection of the initial offer and creates an entirely new offer that can be accepted or rejected. [Last reviewed in August of 2023 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex COMMERCE commercial activities business law contracts wex definitions business sectors commercial transactions Wex Toolbox
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: acceptance | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you acceptance Acceptance means to assent to the terms of an offer . Some common uses of the term “acceptance” in a legal sense include: In the context of contracts , acceptance refers to one person’s compliance with the terms of an offer made by another. Acceptance is judged objectively, but can either be expressly stated or implied by the offeree's conduct. To form a binding contract , acceptance should be relayed in a manner authorized, requested, or at least reasonably expected by the offeror . In the context of insurance law, acceptance takes place when an insurer agrees to the person's application for insurance and in turn issues them a policy to cover certain risks or perils. In the context of business transactions, acceptance takes place when a buyer agrees to purchase a product from a seller even if the product or goods was not what was originally agreed upon. [Last reviewed in June of 2022 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex COMMERCE commercial activities business law contracts commercial transactions legal education and practice wex definitions Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The altered text introduces a structural flaw by placing the Broker Disclosure paragraph at the end, after the buyer's agreement to confidentiality and non-solicitation. This obscures the basis for the agreement and makes the acceptance of the NDA less informed. As Scraped Law Snippet 1 states, an offer must be stated in a way that would lead a reasonable person to expect a binding contract to arise from its acceptance. By delaying the disclosure, the buyer might not fully understand the offer's basis when accepting, weakening the contract's validity.
